mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and tighten financial conditions, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no negative correlation with rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and higher recession probability, causing S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power with a t-stat below 1.5 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized domestic demand growth, which drives corporate earnings expectations higher and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant with a p-value above 0.10 in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth signals improving manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which triggers short-term risk-on positioning and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during inflationary supply bottlenecks, or 5-day forward returns show zero correlation with INDPRO growth rates across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and wage growth, which supports corporate revenue forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth consistently fails to predict positive returns, with a rolling Rank IC below 0.01 over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings crossing above 0.4% trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening and margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets price in inflation pass-through successfully, resulting in positive forward returns in more than 60% of high-inflation months, invalidating the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth combined with stable or declining federal funds rates indicates accommodative monetary policy supporting economic expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal loses predictive accuracy when GDP growth decouples from monetary conditions, or the interaction effect yields a negative Sharpe ratio in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth crossing above 0.5% month-over-month signals robust manufacturing sector momentum and rising business investment, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when production surges coincide with rising input costs, or the threshold signal produces a negative hit rate below 45%.
